title: Recruitee Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Recruitee** is a collaborative Applicant Tracking System (ATS) exposing a REST API under `https://api.recruitee.com/c/{company_id}`. This connector — `RecruiteeConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "recruitee"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Recruitee workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Current User | `/current_user` | Token-owner probe used for `install()` + `health_check()` |
| Candidates | `/candidates` | List, get, create, update, delete candidates |
| Offers (Jobs) | `/offers` | List, get, create job requisitions |
| Departments | `/departments` | List company departments used to scope offers |
| Pipelines | `/pipeline_templates` | List per-company pipeline templates |
| Stages | `/offers/{offer_id}/stages` | List the stages of a specific offer's pipeline |
| Tags | `/tags` | List candidate tags |
| Notes | `/candidates/{id}/notes` | List + create candidate notes |
| Tasks | `/tasks` | List company tasks (assignable to candidates / offers) |
| Hiring Managers | `/admins` | List hiring managers / admins |

The connector normalises Candidates + Offers into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, `Retry-After` honoured), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::RecruiteeHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Recruitee Personal API Token

1. Sign in to https://app.recruitee.com.
2. Open **Settings → Apps & Plugins → Personal API Tokens**.
3. Click **Add new token**, give it a descriptive name (e.g. `Shielva`), and copy the token value. Recruitee shows it only once.
4. From the URL after login (`https://app.recruitee.com/#/companies/{COMPANY_ID}/...`) copy the numeric **Company ID** — it is also visible under **Settings → Company Profile**.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Recruitee**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `company_id` (string, required) | Recruitee Company ID from step 4 |
| `api_token` (secret, required) | Personal API Token from step 3 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.recruitee.com/c` (rarely needed; proxy or sandbox) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 to match Recruitee's standard quota |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `company_id` + `api_token` are present, then probes `GET /current_user` to confirm the token resolves and binds to the company. On 2xx → `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`. On 401/403 → `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /current_user` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Recruitee REST API uses **Personal API Token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>` (standard Bearer prefix — different from Wix).
- `company_id` is **NOT** sent as a header. It is embedded in the URL path: every request goes to `https://api.recruitee.com/c/{company_id}/...`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `company_id` + `api_token` are non-empty. Probes `GET /current_user` to verify the token resolves to the company. Initialises `RecruiteeHTTPClient`. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_token, token_type="api_key")` for surface parity — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /current_user` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. The token lives in `self.config` and is persisted via `save_config()` after install validation. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

No `wix-account-id` / `wix-site-id` analogues — Recruitee is single-tenant per company, scoped via the URL path.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `company_id` | string | yes | `self.company_id` | URL path segment `/c/{company_id}` |
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header value |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.recruitee.com/c`) | Override for proxy / sandbox |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["company_id", "api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://api.recruitee.com/c"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `RecruiteeConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::RecruiteeHTTPClient`; payload shaping lives in `helpers/utils.py`; response transforms live in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `company_id` + `api_token`; probe `GET /current_user` to confirm the token resolves to the company; initialise `RecruiteeHTTPClient`; persist via `save_config`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_token auth — returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_token, token_type="api_key")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /current_user`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/candidates` (offset pagination, page size 100) and `/offers`, normalises each item via `helpers.normalizer`, and ingests via `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found / synced / failed)`.

children.title: Candidates
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /current_user` — returns the API token's owning user.

### `async list_candidates(*, limit=50, offset=0, query=None, sort="by_date", scope="active", status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /candidates` — offset pagination with optional full-text `query`, `sort`, `scope`, `status` filters.

### `async get_candidate(candidate_id) -> Dict`
`GET /candidates/{id}` — full candidate record.

### `async create_candidate(*, name=None, emails=None, phones=None, source=None, offers=None) -> Dict`
`POST /candidates` — body: `{candidate: {name, emails, phones, source}, offers: [{id}, ...]}`. Empty / None fields are stripped so Recruitee defaults apply.

### `async update_candidate(candidate_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /candidates/{id}` — body: `{candidate: {...fields}}`.

### `async delete_candidate(candidate_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /candidates/{id}` — 204 returns `{}`.

children.title: Offers (Jobs)
children.content:
### `async list_offers(*, limit=50, offset=0, status="published", scope="active") -> Dict`
`GET /offers` — list job offers (requisitions).

### `async get_offer(offer_id) -> Dict`
`GET /offers/{id}`.

### `async create_offer(*, title, position_type, employment_type_code="full_time", department_id=None, location_ids=None, description_html="", requirements_html="") -> Dict`
`POST /offers` — body: `{offer: {title, position_type, employment_type_code, description, requirements, department_id?, location_ids?}}`.

children.title: Departments · Pipelines · Stages · Tags · Hiring Managers · Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_departments() -> Dict`
`GET /departments`.

### `async list_pipelines() -> Dict`
`GET /pipeline_templates` — company pipeline templates.

### `async list_stages(offer_id) -> Dict`
`GET /offers/{offer_id}/stages` — stages of the offer's pipeline.

### `async list_tags() -> Dict`
`GET /tags`.

### `async list_tasks(*, limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /tasks`.

### `async list_hiring_managers() -> Dict`
`GET /admins`.

children.title: Notes
children.content:
### `async list_notes(candidate_id) -> Dict`
`GET /candidates/{candidate_id}/notes`.

### `async create_note(candidate_id, body, *, visible_to_team_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /candidates/{candidate_id}/notes` — body: `{note: {body, visible_to_team_id?}}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
RecruiteeError                        # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── RecruiteeAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── RecruiteeBadRequestError          # 400
├── RecruiteeNotFoundError            # 404
├── RecruiteeConflictError            # 409
├── RecruiteeRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── RecruiteeServerError              # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
RecruiteeNetworkError = RecruiteeServerError    # legacy name
RecruiteeNotFound     = RecruiteeNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `max(_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt, Retry-After)` — retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff — retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `RecruiteeNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
404 (company not found) → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)        — logged as warning
5xx / transport → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `company_id` or `api_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Recruitee shows the Personal API Token only once at generation — generate a new one if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)` at install with `Token rejected` | 401 — token revoked or wrong | Verify the token in **Settings → Apps & Plugins → Personal API Tokens** is still active and not for a different company. Re-paste. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)` with `Company not found` | 404 from `/current_user` — wrong `company_id` for this token | Confirm the numeric Company ID under **Settings → Company Profile** matches the company the token belongs to. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — token lacks scope for the endpoint being called | Recruitee Personal API Tokens inherit the owning user's role. Grant the user the right role (e.g. Administrator) in **Settings → Users**. |
| `recruitee.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Recruitee per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `list_stages(offer_id)` | Offer was archived or deleted | Confirm the offer exists via `get_offer(offer_id)` first. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'RecruiteeNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `RecruiteeNetworkError = RecruiteeServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Notes / candidates created via `create_*` are missing from later list calls | Eventual consistency — Recruitee indexes lag a few seconds | Re-query after 2–3 s, or call `get_candidate(id)` for an authoritative read. |
